forest: A pack of 2d4+2 gray wolves stalk the players from afar, slowly but surely following them, awaiting the right moment to strike the weakest of the bunch.

A wounded cervidae (deer sacred to Nylea), who wandered away from her herd, cries for help. Upon inspecting her, the players realize that she has a magical wound under her belly, spreading into the ground like moss—a curse from Pharika.
A sudden arrow flies through the party. A group of leonin hunters emerge from the woods and demand their goods, or else they will kill them.
The party is ambushed by a group of 1d4 angry returned (use zombie stats). They are armed with makeshift weapons and wood pikes.

The forest turns completely silent all of a sudden. If the players start moving, they will hear cracking sounds of wood being broken. Afterwards, a pair of giant ents stop the party and try to capture them.
The party steps in a hidden trap and falls into a cave complex.
A sudden light emerges in front of the players, blinding them for a second. A mysterious oracle in white robes, covering their face behind a scarlet veil, tells them that they must follow them.
The party comes across a water pond, with crystal-clear waters and a little statue of Thassa in the middle. If the players touch the statue, it transforms into a beautiful naiad that blesses them.

The players find a small cabin in a forest glade. There's nobody in the cabin, but there's plenty of beautiful gems and tasty food. If all the players get inside, the cabin suddenly shuts down and starts crushing the players, revealing to be a giant mimic. All the gems and food looted turn to be ash.
A giant roc starts circling the players. Its shadow glooms over their presence. The bird tries to snatch one of the adventurers to take them back to its nest.
A cyclops stops the players and demands a toll to let them pass through. If the players don't pay, he threatens to kill them all.

The players are stopped by the body of a giant snake blocking their path. The snake body extends greatly across the path. If they follow the body, they will find a hole in the ground, which is the lair of the snake.
A pair of vines pull one of the adventurers by the legs, then, one by one they are all snatched by living vines, which drag them to a giant carnivore tree that wants to eat them.
An exotic bird sacred to Karametra is spotted resting on a branch. It's a magical creature that can grant great fortune to those who touch it, and great pain to those who hurt it.
A friendly centaur explorer passes by the players. The explorer can give them directions and advice about traveling in the forest, and also shares knowledge of safe paths.

A loud rumbling sound echoes through the forest. It gets exponentially stronger until it becomes deafening. A giant flash flood emerges from the trees and threatens to drown the party—Thassa's anger made manifest.
The party comes across a dark hole on the ground. It's about 3 square meters and it's extremely dark. Inside, a secret cave that leads to an underground lake inhabited by naiads.
A sudden thunderstorm of magical origin appears, covering the forest. A few minutes afterward, blue-colored fireflies and lampads start flying numbly across the forest. The storm lasts for 1d4 hours.
The party encounters a giant dead tree with a small space carved inside, where the bones of an ancient hero lie, still clutching their weapon.
A pack of 2d4 rabid dogs ambushes the party of adventurers.
The party comes across a large and ancient temple complex dedicated to Nylea, overgrown with sacred vines.
A loud horn trills behind the players, a few moments later, the party is attacked by a group of 2d6 returned hoplites (use zombie stats with spears and shields).
The party stumbles upon a rather large ancient cemetery from forgotten times. The tombstones are small monoliths with runes carved on top. There's a wailing lampad in the middle of the cemetery.
While on the trail, the party accidentally comes across quicksand and gets trapped in it.

A parade of mystical nymphs and satyrs walk across the players. They ignore the party and move gently across the forest. If the players follow them quietly, they will lead them to a hidden grove sacred to Karametra.
The players stumble upon a foul hamlet in a glade. It smells like corpse. If they investigate the houses, they will find 2d4 returned (zombies), which proceed to attack the party.
The sounds of a lyre enchant the forest. If players follow the sound, they will find a friendly satyr, who offers them food, wine, and songs.
A brutish group of 1d4 cyclopes are looking for their next lunch, coming across the players, they attack them.
A sudden wildfire ignites the forest—perhaps Purphoros's rage. It lasts for 1d4 days before extinguishing.
The players find an adventuring party of 1d4 explorers resting. They offer them food, warmth and a place to rest.

The players come across an opposing adventuring party of 1d4 warriors resting. They act friendly and kind, and try to trick the players to rest with them, so that they can loot their resources and treasure.

The party reaches a dead patch of woods, where the grass is decaying and the trees are dead. At the center of the area, a pile of corpses leave a terrible stench—victims of Pharika's plague. If the players touch them, they contract a deadly disease.
A giant ant colony blocks the path of the party. The colony is so large that it's practically a cave system which the players can explore, but inside, giant ants guard the tunnels.
The party finds a tree hollow with a magical spiral inside. If a creature touches the spiral, it sucks them into Nyx itself—a whole new pocket dimension.
From a bush, a swarm of mutant wasps emerges and clouds the sky. The party must hide quickly or get attacked by the swarm.
The party finds a calm river with still and dark water. Underneath the water there's 2d4 giant crabs, waiting to ambush someone.
A giant undead bear charges against the party from the forest bush—a returned beast.
The party hears a loud and brash sound of a falling tree. A rampaging treant starts destroying everything around the party. It seems to be under some sort of dark influence from Erebos.
A blue mist starts emerging from the woods. The adventurers enter a Nyx-touched forest, enchanted and filled with starlight.
A lightning strikes the ground in front of the party—Keranos's power. In the impact crater, there's a strange stone that seems to be emanating powerful magic.

The party finds an abandoned caravan. Overgrown vines hide it among the forest. Inside, there's a man turned into some sort of vine-creature, formed out of branches and leaves. He asks to be freed of this curse from Karametra.
A mysterious pond appears near the party. There's a little island in the middle, where a lampad made out of starlight offers a powerful weapon to the party in exchange for something living.
The party finds a hidden chest inside a tree. They must get it out of the tree to open it. If they cut the tree, it reveals to be an angry treant. The chest has a 50% chance of being empty.
A giant ape, walking on two feet, charges against the party and abducts the smallest member of the party, taking them to its burrow in the depths of the woods.
A large party of 4d6 minotaurs are spotted running across the forest. They seem to have kidnapped a pair of humans and are carrying them as they run. The captives scream for help.
The players run into the aftermath of a battle between a champion of Iroas and a giant. They are both dead, locked in combat. The champion carries a quest contract related to killing the giant.

The party discovers an abandoned military fort in a small glade. There's no one inside, as if everyone just vanished into Nyx. Pieces of armor, clothes and weapons are randomly scattered around the fort.
The party stumbles upon a dangerous mass of quickmud.

An adventurer's pack is left alone on the player's path. It contains useful resources, 1d4 potions of healing, 5d20 gold coins and a little pack of 1d4 gems. Inside, there's a letter stained with blood about paying a debt to Erebos.
An adult green dragon circles the forest skies, looking for its next prey. The party must hide, or become the next target. The lair of the dragon is nearby.

A wandering and gloomy leonin ranger passes by the party. It warns them of a strange danger deep in the woods. The ranger offers guidance to the nearest polis. There's a 50% chance he's leading them to an ambush by thieves.

A rift in reality, like a floating mirror into Nyx, appears in front of the party. An eccentric mage emerges from it and opens another one, leaving these rifts behind. The rifts are portals to the same dimension but at different times.
The day suddenly turns into night. The party hears a distant choir of voices in cacophony. If they follow it, they find a group of cultists of Phenax in the middle of a summoning ritual.
The party stumbles upon an owlbear, eating the remains of a recent prey.
A crow flies in front of the party and stops right ahead. The creature starts speaking to them about something important going on in the woods—a messenger of Erebos.
A large wooden totem to Keranos appears on the path. This totem can grant 3 simple wishes. There's a 50% chance that the wish granted is the contrary to what the player wished.
The party comes across the tree nest of a pack of 2d4 harpies. There's precious gems inside the nest, as well as harpy eggs, ready to hatch.
The woods start to slowly flood. On the nearest body of water, there's 1d4 water elementals wreaking havoc—Thassa's wrath.
The adventurers are stopped by a marching army of hoplites from a nearby polis. They are asked to pay tribute to the war efforts, or pay the consequences.
The players find a lone and mysterious oracle resting at a campfire. They offer food and warmth. The oracle can see into the future and may help one player see their destiny.
The party discovers an ancient temple, overgrown by the forest and hidden beneath trees. There's a small tunnel that leads into an ancient dungeon of forgotten origin, predating the polis.
The players run into a giant tree house. The place is occupied by 3d6+2 returned (zombies).
The party comes across the feeding grounds of a hydra. The monster is nearby, and there's a 1 in 4 chance it ambushes the party.
The party enters a dark and eerie area of the forest. The trees are old, dark and wide, and there's spiderwebs everywhere. If they keep moving, they will find 3d6 giant spiders.
The party finds an abandoned barrel full of quality wine—a gift from Karametra.
The adventurers are stopped by a massive ravine crossing across the forest. There's a wounded dwarf screaming for help on the bottom of the ravine.

You find a giant rat's nest under the roots of a tree. It's filled with precious ornaments. There's 4d4 small baby rats that start crying at the sight of the adventurers. After 5 minutes, 2d8 giant rats reach the nest.
The players are ambushed by 3d4 returned hoplites (use wight stats), each one riding a nightmare. They want to drag the party to the Underworld.
Very strong winds take the party by surprise. If they follow the origin of the winds, they will find a pair of air elementals having a brawl.
The players come across a small glade with large dirt mounds. If they investigate further, they are attacked by 1d4 ankhegs. There's a hidden cave system underneath them.
A dead bird falls from the sky. After a few seconds, dozens more dead birds start raining. The cause is a pair of harpies, looking for their prey.
A pair of centaur hunters stop the party, warning them about a powerful predator in the near area. The party can help the hunters tracking down and killing the predator, which is a powerful chimera.
A warband of 3d4 minotaur raiders rushes past the party on foot. They are running towards a battle against a group of leonin warriors.
The party comes across a large bog in the middle of the forest. There's 4d6 giant frogs scattered around. While not aggressive, they are very territorial.
A sudden green mist starts moving towards the players. The mist is poisonous and deals 1d4 damage every turn—Pharika's curse. It comes from a static death plant, close to the party.
The players run into what seems to be a large maze made out of bush walls, some 4 meters tall. At the center of the maze, there's a secret chest with a very powerful elixir inside.
The party disturbs the sleeping grounds of 2d4 giant bats.

The adventurers come across a little pond of crystal-clear water. The bottom of the pond is only 1 meter deep, and it's made of small blue crystal. Drinking the water grants 3d6 temporary hit points and resistance against necrotic damage for 8 hours—blessed by Thassa.
Entering a dark and old part of the forest, the party runs into a giant fire beetle colony on the surface. There's 1d20+5 beetles guarding the area. They aren't aggressive, but will attack intruders.

The players find a large pine-like tree with very vibrant yellow needles. It produces an exotic golden fruit, about 1d6 of them, similar to a pineapple. Eating it grants the same effects a long rest produces—Karametra's blessing.

The players find a stone statue of a hero in combat pose. Touching it morphs the statue into a living human soldier. The soldier is confused and angry. He has been frozen in time for 500 years by the gods.
The party runs into a beautiful hill, full of colorful flowers. On top of the hill, a unicorn sacred to Karametra protects the land.

The forest suddenly turns darker and smells like rot. They enter a swampy area, obscured by the old trees. There's a nearby cabin, surrounded by a mud pond, where a pair of medusas live. There's a 50% chance that a lampad of Erebos comes to the cabin if the players get inside.
A previous adventuring party is killed by a group of 3d4 bandits, lead by one bandit captain. The players run into the aftermath of the battle, while the bandits are busy stealing the war spoils.

The party finds a very large trail of blonde hair. If they follow it, it takes 1d4 hours to reach its end. A tall abandoned ivory tower. The tower is inhabited by 1d4 displacer beasts, 1d4 specters and one powerful mage corrupted by Phenax, to whom the hair belongs.
The players reach an area with lots of fallen trees and destroyed landmarks. There's tons of rocks all around. A pair of basilisks ambush the party and try to eat them or turn them into stone.
A flock of 2d4 flying snakes descend into the ground, attacking the players. They are defending a nearby nest.
The party comes across a little vertical hole in the ground. Inside, there's a small cave with 2d4 rust monsters.
The players find a little piece of land where it's snowing and freezing. The source seems to be a diamond on a tree—a fragment of Nyx frozen in the mortal realm.

A terrible earthquake shakes the land. Afterwards, a massive purple worm emerges from the ground, destroying everything left and right. It seems to be blinded by some sort of magically induced rage from Purphoros.
A pegasus circles above the trees. The celestial creature is looking for a returned champion (use wight stats). The players can help the pegasus find it.

The party hears distant screeching noises and echoing wails. If they investigate (DC 17), they spot a nightmare stalking them. If not, the nightmare appears during the first long rest they take, or at night.

On the path of the party, they spot a distant watchtower on top of a hill. It's abandoned, except for a few 2d4 pesky giant rats. There's a lot of useful items inside, and they can even use it as a refuge.

The party hears a loud celestial voice commanding them to stop. Afterwards, an avatar of Heliod (use deva stats) manifests next to the party, and explains that they are about to enter a cursed land. A demon band sent by Erebos have taken residency deep in the area. The party can convince the avatar to help in killing the demons.

An ancient bronze dragon spots the party from the sky. He lands near them and starts talking about how the forest has changed over the centuries. The dragon won't leave, as he's really excited about talking and sharing wisdom.
The players find an abnormally large dead bear. He's dead and has its stomach wide open. Inside, there's 3 common martial weapons and an explorer's pack.
A crying cyclops is spotted by the party. He's next to the corpse of another cyclops, which has a giant axe piercing his skull. There's a weird haze of Nyx energy surrounding the area.

The party finds a magnificent and massive abandoned ancient city. Its buildings are made out of marble and limestone. The city is mostly abandoned, except for a sphinx, living in the center, where a temple to Kruphix is found.

The adventurers come across an abandoned stone temple, covered in vines and moss. The entrance is guarded by one gorgon (the bull creature). Inside, there's various useful items, weapons and treasure, but it's guarded by 1d4 medusas scattered around various rooms.

The earth shakes, trees fall and the sky roars. A hydra of legendary size (use stats of your choice, or a young dragon with multiple heads) is near the party. It's wreaking havoc and consuming anything that it sees, living or dead—perhaps awakened by the gods themselves.
roads: A traveling merchant's wagon has broken a wheel. The grateful merchant offers a discount on wares (healing potions, rations, simple weapons) if the party helps with repairs.
A lone satyr sits by the roadside playing a melancholic tune on pan pipes. He's mourning a lost love and will share wine and stories if the party listens.
1d4 leonin scouts approach peacefully, asking if the party has seen any Returned on the roads. They share information about safe camping spots.
A returned palamnite (use zombie stats) wanders aimlessly in the road, confused and lost. It's not immediately hostile but will defend itself if threatened.
The party finds a small roadside shrine to Karametra. Praying here grants inspiration to one character until their next long rest.
2d4 bandits demand a toll of 10 gold per person to pass. They're poorly equipped and will flee if two of them fall.
A priest of Ephara travels with two acolytes, offering blessings and news from the nearest polis. They warn of increased Returned activity.
A minotaur sits alone, sharpening his axe. He's on a personal quest and ignores the party unless provoked.
A sudden dust devil whirls across the plains, revealing it be a minor air elemental (use dust mephit stats). It's playful rather than hostile.
The road passes an ancient battlefield. 1d4 skeletons rise from the earth but collapse after one round of combat—echoes of past glory.
A noble on horseback with 2 guards passes by, haughty and dismissive. If treated with respect, they share valuable information about the road ahead.
The party finds a beautiful vista overlooking the sea or plains. Resting here for an hour grants the benefits of a short rest in half the time.
1d6 giant vultures circle overhead, then land near the party. They're scavenging a dead horse but will attack if the party approaches too close.
A pegasus flies overhead, its rider—a champion of Heliod—scanning the horizon. They land briefly to warn of danger ahead (roll again for an encounter in 1d4 hours).
The party meets a group of 1d4+1 commoners fleeing their village. They speak of a 'great beast' terrorizing their home (actually just a large boar).
A roadside inn appears, run by a friendly cyclops named Boros. He offers food and rest at reasonable prices and loves to arm-wrestle.
2d4 stirges burst from tall grass beside the road, drawn by the scent of blood and sweat.
The party finds a discarded shield bearing the symbol of a polis. It's serviceable but clearly abandoned after a battle.
An oracle (use druid stats) offers to read one character's fate for 5 gold. The reading is cryptic but genuinely helpful.
1d4 harpies perch on ancient stone pillars, singing their luring song. They flee if reduced to half hit points.
The road crosses a shallow stream. In the water, a naiad (use water elemental stats, but friendly) asks for help removing debris from her stream.
The party encounters 2d4 goats blocking the road, belonging to a nearby shepherd who's fallen asleep under a tree.
A sudden rainstorm strikes (if Thassa is displeased). The road becomes muddy and difficult terrain for 1d4 hours.
An athlete training for the games jogs past, offering a friendly wave and a blessing of Iroas.
The party finds a milestone marker defaced with warnings about 'the gray merchant' (a local legend/ghost story).
1d4 giant lizards sun themselves on rocks near the road. They're not aggressive unless attacked.
A traveling philosopher (use noble stats) engages the party in debate about the nature of the gods. He's annoying but harmless.
The party discovers a small cave off the road containing 1d4 giant bats and a small cache of 2d10 gold pieces.
2d6 zombies (Returned) shamble across the road. They wear ancient armor and seem to be marching toward something.
A priest of Erebos sits at a crossroads, offering last rites to travelers for a donation. He's somber but not evil.
The party meets a centaur scout who shares news of the wilds and offers to guide them to a shortcut (saves 1d4 hours of travel).
A flock of blood hawks (2d4) attacks, driven mad by hunger.
The road passes through an ancient stone archway covered in vines. Passing through grants a sense of peace and removes one level of exhaustion.
1d4 cultists of Phenax try to swindle the party with a fake 'blessing' or cursed item. They flee if confronted.
A giant boar charges from the scrubland, angry and territorial. It has 1d4 piglets nearby it's protecting.
The party finds a roadside memorial to fallen heroes. Paying respects grants advantage on the next saving throw.
2d4 kobolds have set up a 'toll booth' using sticks and rocks. They're more comical than threatening.
A warhorse without a rider trots along the road, saddled but riderless. Its saddlebags contain rations and 1d20 gold pieces.
The party encounters a lampad (use dryad stats) who emerges from a rocky outcrop, curious about travelers.
A traveling bard offers to join the party for a day, sharing songs and stories. He knows valuable rumors.
1d6 giant wolf spiders emerge from burrows beside the road, defending their territory.
The party finds an abandoned camp with still-warm coals. Investigating reveals tracks leading into the plains (DM's choice of what left them).
A dust storm reduces visibility to 30 feet for 1d4 hours. Navigation becomes difficult.
2d4 giant centipedes swarm from beneath a fallen pillar the party passes.
The party meets a veteran returning from war, who shares tactical advice and offers to spar with the party's fighters.
A swarm of ravens follows the party for 1d4 hours—an omen from Erebos, though not necessarily negative.
The road passes a vineyard. The owner offers free wine samples and sells provisions at fair prices.
1d4 giant scorpions lurk in rocky terrain beside the road, ambushing travelers.
A unicorn appears briefly in the distance—a blessing from Karametra. The next meal the party eats is especially nourishing (gain 1d4 temporary hit points).
The party finds a broken statue of a god. Repairing it (DC 12 mason's tools or Mending spell) grants a minor blessing (+1 to AC until next dawn).
2d4 bandits led by a bandit captain attempt an ambush from roadside rocks. They surrender if their captain falls.
A giant eagle circles overhead, watching the party. If they're in danger within the next day, it may intervene once.
The party encounters a funeral procession of 2d6 commoners taking a body to be interred. They're somber but welcoming.
A satyr reveler and 1d4 commoners are having a roadside party. They invite the party to join and share food and wine.
1d4 giant toads hop across the road from a nearby marsh or irrigation ditch.
A discarded spear stuck in the ground. It's a serviceable weapon but nothing special.
A messenger on horseback races past, carrying urgent news between polis. They don't stop.
The road passes an olive grove sacred to Karametra. Resting here feels especially peaceful.
2d4 giant rats infest an abandoned roadside structure, protecting a nest with 3d10 silver pieces.
A griffon lands nearby to drink from a stream, ignoring the party unless they approach within 30 feet.
The party meets 1d4 scouts from a polis, mapping the roads. They share information about nearby locations.
A will-o'-wisp appears at dusk, leading travelers astray. It's mischievous rather than murderous and will flee if attacked.
The party finds a roadside fountain that produces clean, cold water—a gift from the gods.
1d6 stirges and 1d4 giant centipedes have made a nest in roadside ruins.
A bronze scout (use animated armor stats) patrols the road, asking travelers their business. It's programmed to protect against threats.
A druid tending to injured wildlife offers healing (1 free cure wounds spell) in exchange for a donation to nature.
2d6 skeletons (ancient warriors) rise from roadside graves but only attack if the party disturbs their rest.
A giant constrictor snake crosses the road, hunting. It's not immediately hostile but will defend itself.
The party finds a battlefield where 1d4 animated swords (use flying sword stats) still 'fight' against invisible enemies.
A priest of Heliod offers blessings and healing to travelers. He asks for news of the road ahead.
1d4 cockatrices strut along the roadside, pecking at the ground. They're territorial but can be avoided.
A gladiator traveling to compete in games is friendly and offers to share combat techniques.
A sudden blessing of Heliod causes the sun to shine especially bright, granting inspiration to one character.
2d4 wolves follow the party at a distance, curious but not hostile unless the party appears weak.
The road passes through a field of wildflowers. A swarm of insects (bees) defends their hive if disturbed.
A giant elk with golden antlers watches the party from a hilltop—a servant of Nylea. It's a good omen.
The party finds a merchant's corpse beside the road, killed by bandits. His goods (worth 3d10 gold) are scattered.
1d4 giant poisonous snakes sun themselves on warm rocks beside the road.
A traveling smith has set up a temporary forge at a crossroads, offering repairs and simple weapons for sale.
A manticore flies overhead. It circles once, decides they're not worth the trouble, and flies away.
A roadside altar to Purphoros smokes with eternal flames. Forging or repairing metal here grants a +1 bonus for 24 hours.
2d4 giant crabs have wandered inland from the coast, confused and aggressive.
A champion of Iroas (use knight stats) seeks worthy opponents for honorable sparring.
A sudden phase spider appears briefly, hunting. It attacks once then phases away if it takes any damage.
The road passes an ancient amphitheater where 1d4 specters of performers still 'act' their eternal play.
A traveling healer offers medical services and sells healing potions at a 10% markup.
1d4 harpies nest in roadside cliffs, singing but not attacking unless the party climbs toward their nest.
The party finds a lost child who has wandered from a nearby farm. Reuniting them earns gratitude and a reward.
A gorgon (the bull, not medusa) grazes in the distance. It ignores the party unless they approach within 60 feet.
The road passes a training ground where 2d4 guards practice. They're friendly and offer to spar.
A nightmare gallops past in the distance, its rider cloaked and mysterious. It doesn't stop.
The party encounters a sphinx (use gynosphinx stats) resting on ancient ruins. It asks one riddle and lets them pass if answered correctly.
A chimera flies overhead, hunting. It spots the party but decides to pursue easier prey (50% chance it attacks if provoked).
The road passes a sacred grove where a unicorn and 1d4 dryads dwell. They're peaceful but protective of their home.
A bronze sable (use warhorse stats with immunity to poison) pulls a chariot carrying a champion of Heliod who offers blessings.
The party finds an ancient treasure chest buried beside the road (DC 15 Investigation). It contains 4d10 gold pieces and one random uncommon magic item.
A medusa disguised as a beautiful traveler asks to join the party for safety. She reveals her nature only if attacked (she's actually trying to reform).
The road passes through a valley where a young bronze dragon lairs. It's curious and wants to talk, not fight.
A hydra emerges from a ravine near the road, hungry and aggressive. This is a serious threat.
The party witnesses a god's manifestation—choose one appropriate to your campaign. The god offers a quest, blessing, or warning before vanishing.
sea: A pod of 1d4 merrow (use merrow stats, CR 2) surface around the boat, hurling barbed harpoons. They retreat if half their number falls.
A lone triton scout (CR 1/2) hails the party in Primordial, warning of a sahuagin warband ahead and offering to guide them to safety for a pearl or favor.
The wind dies completely. For 1d4 hours the sea is glassy and silent — sailors whisper that Thassa is watching. A DC 12 Wisdom (Survival) check rows the becalmed vessel to a current.
A massive shadow passes under the hull. Moments later a young kraken tendril (treat as one tentacle of a kraken, AC 20, 20 HP, +17 to hit, 3d6+10 bludgeoning) reaches up to grasp a sailor.
A school of 2d6 hunter sharks (CR 2 each) circles a wounded sailor in the water. They will turn on the party if blood is spilled.
A giant octopus (CR 1) clings to the side of the ship, dragging cargo into the deep. Cutting it off requires a DC 13 Strength check.
A ghost ship of the Returned drifts alongside — its crew of 4d4 zombies (CR 1/4) shamble across boarding planks at moonrise.
A wandering priest of Thassa offers safe passage in exchange for the party pouring a vial of wine into the sea every morning. Breaking the pact summons a water elemental (CR 5).
A sahuagin raiding party (4 sahuagin CR 1/2 + 1 sahuagin priestess CR 2) boards from a stolen skiff. They flee if the priestess falls.
The sea suddenly turns blood-red for a mile in every direction — an omen of Mogis. Crew morale drops; DC 13 Wisdom save or gain a level of exhaustion from despair.
A pegasus skims low over the waves, exhausted. If rescued and watered, it carries a message back to a polis from a grateful champion of Heliod.
A whirlpool yawns open ahead (DC 15 Strength (Athletics) by the helmsman to steer clear, or 4d6 bludgeoning to the hull and a save vs. capsizing).
A dead leviathan the size of a temple drifts past, half-eaten. Inside its ribcage lies an undisturbed reliquary worth 500 gp — and 1d4 ghouls (CR 1) gorged on its flesh.
A friendly merfolk trader (use commoner stats, swim 40) bobs alongside the boat with a net of pearls, coral, and one preserved scroll of water breathing.
Three triton honor-guards (CR 1/2 each) demand the party submit to inspection — Thassa's law forbids carrying iron weapons over the deep without leave.
A sudden squall (storm of Thassa). DC 14 Constitution saves on deck for everyone unsecured or be swept overboard. Lasts 1d4 hours.
A swarm of 1d4 swarms of quippers (CR 1 each) churns the water around any creature that falls in.
A reef of singing coral lures the helmsman; DC 14 Wisdom save or steer the ship onto rocks for 6d10 hull damage.

A young bronze dragon (CR 8) — too tough for low parties — surfaces and engages the party in polite conversation about the weather. Avoid combat at level <5; treat as social if the party is courteous.
A barrel marked with the symbol of Phenax floats by. Inside: 2d4 vials of poison (basic) and a forged map to a 'lost temple' that leads into a Returned ambush.
A pair of harpies (CR 1) wheel down from a cliffside roost to pick off lookouts. They are easily driven off with sustained ranged fire.
A drowned hero (use wight stats, CR 3) walks across the seabed and climbs up the anchor chain at midnight, seeking his lost crown.
A merchant galley signals for aid — they are fending off 6 sahuagin (CR 1/2). Helping earns 100 gp and a bottle of fine Meletian wine.
A pillar of starlight from Nyx falls into the sea half a league away. Investigating leads to a drifting hunk of crystallized Nyx-stuff worth 1d6 × 100 gp to the right buyer.
The hull strikes a submerged statue of a forgotten god. Lifting it (DC 16 Strength group check) reveals the statue weeps freshwater — a minor relic.
A leonin sailor in the crow's nest spots an island that is not on any chart. It is the back of a sleeping aspect of Thassa — landing wakes her (avoid).
A naiad surfaces and bargains for a kiss in exchange for a tide that will speed the ship by a full day's travel.
A bottle washes up on deck with a frantic plea from a sailor marooned three islands south. The bottle is dated four years ago.
A trio of nereids in mourning veils ask to bury a dead champion at sea. They reward respectful aid with a charm of water breathing (1 use).
Ten miles of sea are choked with floating Nyx-petals. Sleeping on the deck during this passage grants prophetic dreams (one true vision of the future).
A storm giant (CR 13) rides past on a thundercloud. Far too dangerous to fight at any level under 12 — treat as awe-inspiring spectacle or brief boon.
Crab boats from a nearby village are under attack by a giant crab (CR 1/8) the size of a wagon (treat as reef shark for stats). Saving them earns local goodwill.
A drifting raft holds two starving castaways (commoners). One is a cultist of Phenax in disguise — DC 15 Insight to spot the lie before he poisons the water cask.
Bioluminescent jellyfish make the sea glow turquoise at night. Touching them: DC 12 Constitution save or 1d6 poison and paralyzed 1 minute.
A pirate galley flying no colors closes fast — 8 bandits (CR 1/8) + 1 bandit captain (CR 2). They demand half the cargo or board.
A waterlogged hoplite shield drifts by, etched with the crest of a polis thought destroyed a century ago.
A young triton noble has eloped with a human sailor and asks to be smuggled to the polis. Tritons (CR 1/2 × 4) pursue them within a day.
A reef of glass-like coral cuts the sea like a labyrinth. Navigating it: DC 14 Intelligence (Navigator's tools) or 4d6 hull damage.
A pod of dolphins escorts the ship for a day — a blessing of Thassa. While they swim with the party, sailors gain advantage on Constitution saves vs. exhaustion.
A dead sea serpent lies coiled on a rocky outcrop. Its venom sacs are intact: 1d4 doses of serpent venom (1d10 poison, DC 11) worth 100 gp each.
A storm pushes the ship into a fog bank. Inside, sound carries strangely; voices call out names of the dead. DC 14 Wisdom save or be lured overboard chasing a voice.
A friendly cyclops fisherman (use cyclops CR 6 but non-hostile) sits on a sea stack hauling nets. He trades fish and rumors for wine.
A returned naval captain (use wight CR 3) walks the deck of his sunken trireme on the seabed — visible by lantern if the party dives. He guards a sealed war chest.
A swarm of stirges (CR 2) descends on the ship at dusk, drawn by the lanterns.
A floating temple to Thassa — a coral platform — rises with the tide. Pilgrims here will heal one party member of any disease in exchange for a vow.
A nereid sings a lament that grants 1d6 temporary HP to all who hear it, and a vivid dream of a drowned city worth investigating.
A merrow chieftain (use merrow CR 2 with 60 HP) challenges the strongest party member to single combat at the prow. Refusal means full assault by 1d4+2 merrow.
A becalmed merchant ship signals SOS — they have been adrift three weeks. Aid earns a 50% discount letter at a major Meletian merchant house.
A creature like a giant nautilus (use giant octopus stats with 50 ft swim) surfaces curiously and follows the ship for an hour, harmless unless attacked.
The party sails past a rock shaped exactly like a face of Kruphix. Stopping to ask one question yields one cryptic true answer (DM choice).
A sphinx (CR 11 — far too tough under level 8) hovers above a rocky island and offers safe passage in exchange for a riddle answered. Wrong answer = magical wind drives the ship a day off course.
A returned ship of the line (1 wight captain CR 3, 4 zombie marines CR 1/4, and 2 specters CR 1) emerges from fog at dusk.
A pair of giant seahorses (treat as warhorse with swim 60) carry triton messengers seeking aid against sahuagin.
A tidal wave warning: in 1 hour, the sea drains away. Anyone not on high ground takes 8d6 bludgeoning from the returning wave. DC 16 Athletics to swim through.
Sirens (use harpy stats with Charm Person 1/day) on a rocky islet try to lure the ship onto the rocks with song. DC 13 Wisdom save vs. the song.

A floating shrine of olive branches and lit candles drifts by — a funeral for a sea-priest. Lighting one's own candle and setting it adrift grants the next long rest the benefit of a 1st-level spell slot recovered (one-time).
The water beneath the ship turns warm and slightly sweet — Karametra's tear, said to ease seasickness for all aboard for a tenday.
A leviathan calf (use young dragon stats, water-themed) swims under the ship for an hour, harmless but terrifying.
A pirate hoplite (use thug CR 1/2) leaps aboard from a rope swung from a hidden skiff — distraction while 3 more pirates climb the stern.
The party finds a drifting jar of ancient olive oil, blessed by an oracle — drinking a sip restores 1d4+1 HP, single use.

A massive tortoise the size of a galley surfaces and asks (in Primordial) to be scratched on the shell. Rewards: a single pearl of power (very rare) for very polite parties at high level, otherwise a fine pearl worth 100 gp.
A storm drives the ship onto an unknown shore — start of an island side-quest (DM seed).
The skies turn green and lightning strikes the mast (DC 13 Dex save by helmsman or 4d10 lightning damage to the ship and 2d10 to anyone on deck) — Keranos's wrath.
A small fleet of triton merchants offers exotic wares: pearl-strung armor (treat as studded leather, 50 gp), waterproof bags, and one potion of water breathing per ship.
A bone-white whale breaches near the ship and vanishes; sailors mutter that whoever sees it last will die before the next moon (no mechanical effect — atmosphere).
A drowned satyr (use ghost stats, CR 4) clings to the rudder, asking the party to deliver his lyre to his lover in a coastal village.
A pair of swarms of poisonous snakes (CR 2 each) wash aboard on a piece of driftwood.
A swarm of seabirds shrieks overhead, dropping fresh fish on the deck. Lasts 10 minutes — rations for the whole crew for a day.
A coral reef glimmers below; a DC 13 Investigation check finds a sunken trireme with 2d4 × 50 gp in goods, guarded by 1 giant moray eel (use giant constrictor snake CR 2 with swim).
The party sights a single hoplite walking on the surface of the sea, miles from any shore — an avatar of Heliod testing their resolve. He vanishes if approached.
A massive bronze chain runs from the seabed up into the clouds. Climbing it: DC 16 Athletics. It leads to a forgotten temple-island of Purphoros (DM hook).
A floating market on lashed-together rafts welcomes the party — 1d4 fair stalls, news, and 1 magic item under 500 gp available.
A returned siren (use banshee CR 4) wails across the water — anyone within 60 ft makes a DC 13 Con save or take 3d6 psychic damage.
A giant turtle-shell drifts past, large enough to use as a coracle. Sleeping in it grants advantage on saves vs. drowning for 24 hours (Thassa's favor).
A sea-witch in a coracle hails the party — she trades potions (1d4 random common potions) for fresh meat or a lock of hair.
A storm cloud takes the shape of a frowning face. For the next hour, any spellcasting on deck requires a DC 13 concentration check against ambient divine static — Keranos is displeased.
A friendly water elemental (CR 5 but non-hostile) rises beside the ship and pushes it for a day, halving travel time.
A trireme of leonin marines hails the party — they offer to escort the ship to port in exchange for sharing rumors.
The party witnesses a battle between a giant shark (CR 5) and a pair of merrow on the surface a quarter mile off.
Floating chunks of pumice mark a recent volcanic eruption; the air smells of sulfur. Sailing through: DC 12 Con save or be poisoned 1 hour.
A diver surfaces with an ancient bronze helm bearing the seal of a king from Meletis's founding era — worth 250 gp to a temple of Ephara.
A ghost-light dances on the prow at night for three nights running — a herald of Erebos. Each night, one sailor must succeed DC 12 Wis save or have nightmares (no long-rest benefit).
A pod of 2d4 reef sharks (CR 1/2) trails the ship for offal. Harmless unless the party swims.
A pair of nereids ask the party to deliver a wreath to a drowned hero's family on shore. Reward: a charm that calms one storm.
The wind shifts and carries the scent of burning olive trees from over the horizon — a coastal village is under attack (DM hook).
A merrow priestess (CR 2 with cleric spells) parleys for the release of a captured triton. She offers a coral comb worth 50 gp in trade.
A sea-cyclops (use cyclops CR 6) wades thigh-deep in shallows ahead, hurling boulders at any passing ship. Avoid or fight from range.
The hull strikes a submerged mast; the ship takes 2d10 damage. Repair: DC 15 carpenter's tools and 4 hours.
A friendly oracle of Thassa, walking on the waves, blesses one party member with advantage on their next saving throw.
A floating cage holds a bound nereid — sahuagin prisoner. Freeing her: she gifts a single 'one breath underwater' charm to each rescuer.
A vast school of mackerel boils the surface — perfect catch, +1 week of rations for the ship.
A pair of returned hoplites (use skeleton CR 1/4) climb the anchor chain at dawn, dripping seaweed.
A whirlpool opens briefly, revealing a glimpse of a sunken polis — Olantin, the lost city. Charts found here are worth 200 gp to scholars.
A friendly pirate (commoner with rapier) offers to sell the party a treasure map for 50 gp. 50% chance it leads to loot, 50% to a trap.
A drowning child (use commoner) clings to a barrel — rescue earns a vague prophecy from her oracle mother on shore.
A bronze ram on a sunken trireme is recoverable (DC 17 Strength group check) and worth 400 gp to a war temple.
A school of dolphins drives a wounded shark (CR 2) right against the hull — easy kill, fresh meat.
A wandering aspect of Thassa (use young water elemental CR 5 reskinned) tests the captain with three questions about the sea. Pass: ship gains +1 to navigation checks for a year.
A storm tosses up a chest from the deep — 200 gp in coin, soaked but intact, and one potion of greater healing.
A lone Returned dragging itself across the seabed reaches the side of the ship at low tide. Easy kill, but it carries a sealed letter to a noble in the next polis.
polis: A herald in scarlet announces that the archon will speak at noon — the party is jostled by a growing crowd. Pickpockets (1d4 thugs, CR 1/2) work the press; DC 14 Perception to notice.
Two rival philosophers argue loudly in the agora. Joining the debate (DC 13 Intelligence check) earns the party a free meal at a wealthy patron's table.
A funeral procession for a fallen hoplite winds through the street. Pausing to honor the dead grants Karametra's small blessing: +1 to next Charisma check in this polis.
A market vendor accuses the party of theft in front of guards (2 guards CR 1/8). DC 14 Persuasion or Sleight of Hand to defuse — otherwise a fine of 10 gp or a night in the cells.
A street oracle, drunk and barefoot, grabs a player's hand and recites a true prophecy in three lines (DM choice).
A noble's chariot races through a crowded street, nearly trampling a child. The party can intervene (DC 13 Dex) to save the child and earn the noble's enmity.
A festival of Iroas has filled the streets — wine, dancing, and arm-wrestling. Winning a contest (DC 15 Athletics) gains 25 gp and local fame.
A masked cultist of Phenax slips a coded note into a party member's pocket and vanishes into the crowd. The note is a meeting invitation at midnight.
A trio of leonin mercenaries (CR 1 each) recruit at a tavern, offering 50 gp each to escort a caravan two days east.
A child runs up clutching a torn scroll — a frantic plea for help from a sibling kidnapped by a Returned cult three streets over.
Twelve hoplite city guards (use guard CR 1/8, with sergeant veteran CR 3) close off the agora — a fugitive is loose. Random searches; contraband questions.
A poet in the agora performs a satire mocking the archon. The crowd cheers; magistrates are coming (1 noble + 2 guards).
A wealthy merchant offers a generous purse (200 gp) to discreetly retrieve a love letter from a rival's villa — petty theft, but the rival employs ex-hoplite guards.

A street prophet of Heliod points at one party member and declares them 'marked for greatness or ruin.' The crowd takes notice; the party gains either advantage or disadvantage on the next social check in this polis (DM choice).
A bronze automaton (use animated armor CR 1, but ornate) wanders the temple plaza, distributing flowers as part of a religious rite.
A black-market dealer offers a curious magical trinket for 100 gp — 50% genuine common magic item, 50% cursed bauble.
A theater is staging a satyr play. Free seating, but a riot is forming outside between two playwright factions.
A philosopher challenges the party to a dialectic over wine. Hold your own (DC 14 Intelligence) for a contact in the local academy.
A polis assembly is voting on whether to send hoplites against a neighboring city. Speaking persuasively (DC 16 Persuasion) can sway the vote.
A famous tattooist offers to ink one party member a sacred mark of their chosen god for free — but it comes with a vision the wearer cannot ignore.
A returned (zombie CR 1/4) stumbles out of an alley in broad daylight, panicking the crowd. Two guards arrive in 1d4 rounds.
A noble's banquet is open to wandering heroes who can recite a famous deed. Free food, wine, and a chance to meet patrons.
An informant offers, for 20 gp, the names of three thugs who attacked the party — or seem to know they will be attacked tonight.
A satyr troupe plays pan pipes in the square. Joining the dance grants inspiration; ignoring them earns a small mocking insult-poem in the day's gossip.
A bronze statue of a forgotten king weeps olive oil. Pilgrims gather; a priest of Karametra asks the party to investigate.
A duel of honor is being fought in the agora — two hoplites (gladiator stats CR 5 each) circle. Bystanders are taking bets.
A wealthy patron offers 500 gp for the party to attend a banquet incognito and identify a poisoner before dessert.
A priest of Erebos in dark robes hands the party a heavy bronze coin — 'you will need this before the moon turns.' (DM hook).
A child sells olive cakes; eating one grants a tiny boon — advantage on the next saving throw vs. fear in this polis.
A masked man (assassin CR 8 — far too tough under level 5) trails the party for two streets, then melts away. He works for a noble who envies them.
A famous sphinx oracle (CR 11) holds court in a high temple — only the worthy may ask a question. Avoid combat; treat as social.
A mob of 4d6 commoners is gathering to demand bread from a hoarding magistrate. The party can broker peace (DC 15 Persuasion) or be swept into a riot.
A magistrate offers a bounty: 100 gp per ear of a bandit captain plaguing the road south.
A returned hoplite (skeleton CR 1/4) is on display in a magus's window — animated by a clockwork heart, harmless unless touched.
A pickpocket child (commoner) lifts a coinpurse and runs; chasing through alleys is a 3-round skill challenge (Athletics/Acrobatics DC 13 each).
A wandering monk of Kruphix offers to teach one martial maneuver in exchange for the party fasting for 24 hours.
A festival of Purphoros — blacksmiths give free repair on one weapon or piece of armor.
A wealthy noblewoman, veiled, asks the party to escort her secretly out of the polis. She is fleeing an arranged marriage to a cruel archon.
A returned cult is preaching openly at the docks; guards do nothing. Investigating leads to a hidden shrine with 2d4 cultists (CR 1/8) and 1 cult fanatic (CR 2).
A famous bard offers to immortalize one party member in song for 50 gp — the song gives advantage on Charisma checks in any polis for 1 month.
A fire breaks out in a tenement (DC 14 Athletics to drag people clear, 1d4 commoners need saving). Heroism is rewarded with city favor.
A bronze gate slams shut — a citywide curfew is called due to a plague rumor. Anyone caught outside is questioned and possibly held.
A leonin warrior-poet challenges the party member with the highest Strength to an arm-wrestling match — 20 gp stakes (DC 15 Strength).
A magus's shop has a glowing crystal on display, free to anyone who can solve a riddle posted on the door (DC 14 Intelligence).
A priest of Ephara invites the party to witness a magical contract being sworn — they will need witnesses who can read.
A traveling merchant from another polis offers exotic goods at a 25% markup but rare items: 1 random uncommon magic item under 500 gp.
A satyr drunkenly bumps a noble in the street, spilling wine on his toga. The noble demands a hoplite duel; the party can intervene.
A street performer swallows fire (DC 15 to spot it's actual minor magic) for tips.
A philosopher publicly accuses a senator of being a Returned in disguise. The senator's hoplites (2 guards CR 1/8, 1 veteran CR 3) drag him off.
A wealthy dilettante hires the party for 100 gp to attend his symposium as 'wandering heroes' to impress his peers — purely social.
A wandering oracle drops a small bronze key at a player's feet and vanishes. The key fits a door discovered three days from now (DM seed).
A priest of Thassa requests the party escort a sacred chalice to a coastal shrine — 100 gp, two days' travel.
A magistrate's clerk is caught stealing temple funds — the party can blackmail (50 gp/month) or turn him in (10 gp reward).
A leonin smith offers to forge a custom weapon (martial, 50% over list price, gleaming finish) within a week.
A street magus performs minor illusions in the agora. He recognizes one party member as a spell-touched mortal and offers a free identify casting on one item.
A funeral pyre in the harbor — a hero of the polis returned from the dead has finally been laid to rest. Attending grants a small blessing of Heliod (one daily reroll of a Charisma check for a week).
A nightclub of revelers spills into the street, sweeping a party member into a wild dance. DC 12 Constitution save or wake up the next morning with no memory and missing 1d10 gp.
A hooded figure presses a sealed letter into a party member's hand — 'do not read it until you are alone' — and disappears. (DM seed: contains a true secret about a noble.)
A returned hoplite phalanx (use 4 skeletons CR 1/4) marches in formation down a back street; the captain (wight CR 3) leads. They are searching for a deserter.
A famous chef offers a feast in exchange for a tale; eating his food grants +1 HP per HD for the next long rest.
A noble's son challenges the party to a dice game (DC 12 Sleight of Hand to detect his cheating) — 10 gp per round.
A theatergoer faints in the street — under his cloak he carries a magus's spellbook and a knife. He awakes amnesiac. (DM hook.)
A bronze door in the temple of Kruphix opens for the party only — Kruphix sees something in them. Inside: a single question may be asked of the silent god.
A wandering monk of Athreos asks for an obol to cross the river of souls — refusing draws bad luck (disadvantage on next death save).
A baker mistakes a party member for a long-lost cousin and presses fresh bread on them, refusing payment.
A nightingale-voiced singer in a low tavern is actually a nymph (CR 1) in hiding from a jealous god. Befriending her grants a single boon when called upon.
A street brawl erupts (4 commoners + 2 thugs CR 1/2). Joining either side has reputation consequences.
A pickpocket caught red-handed turns out to be a Returned child (zombie CR 1/4 with intact memories). She begs to be freed before guards come.
A festival of Nylea brings garlanded animals through the streets — touching the sacred white bull grants advantage on the next Nature check.
A magus offers the party 25 gp each to test a new potion (effects: random common potion, 25% chance of mild side effect).
A blind oracle in the agora gives one true cryptic line to the first party member who drops a coin in her bowl.
A returned priest (use cult fanatic CR 2) is preaching from a rooftop; the crowd is restless.
A hoplite recruiter offers the party signing bonuses (50 gp) to join a brief campaign — 2 weeks of work, modest pay, real risk.
A noble's wife asks the party to deliver a love letter to a satyr lover in the city pleasure quarter — discreetly.
The polis museum displays a sealed urn said to contain a fragment of Nyx. A scholar will pay 500 gp to anyone who can describe what's inside without opening it.
A pair of leonin twins challenge the party to a footrace through the agora — 25 gp purse (DC 14 Athletics).
A wandering merchant offers the party tattoos that act as minor magical wards (treat as +1 AC bonus for 24 hours, 50 gp, one-time use).
A returned ambassador arrives at the polis gates, demanding parley. The archon's hoplites refuse him; the situation is tense.
A philosopher famous for his hatred of the gods is being arrested for blasphemy — the party can intervene.
A street vendor sells skewers of grilled octopus; one in twenty conceals a rolled-up coded message from the underworld syndicate.
A noble offers the party use of his villa for the night in exchange for 'protection' from a rival's hired thug (1 assassin CR 8 — too tough under level 5; downgrade as needed).
A priest of Heliod conducts a free healing in the agora — one party member can be restored to full HP, but must endure a brief sermon.
A wandering bard recognizes one party member from a song he once heard about them and buys them drinks all night.
A festival of Mogis (forbidden in most poleis) is being held in secret in the underdocks — bloody, dangerous, and well-paid for fighters (50 gp per round won).
A magus's apprentice has accidentally summoned a magma mephit (CR 1/2) into the workshop — the party hears screams from the second floor.
A returned playwright haunts the theater, scribbling his unfinished plays in chalk on the walls each night. Reading them grants inspiration but uneasy dreams.
A wealthy widow offers 300 gp for the party to attend her husband's wake and confirm he is truly dead (he is — and Returned — and quietly raiding her cellar).
A leonin sage offers private tutoring in any one tool proficiency for 200 gp and one week of full-time study.
A bronze automaton (use animated armor CR 1) glitches in the middle of a street, swinging at passersby. Disabling it: DC 14 Intelligence (Arcana).
A noble girl, escaped from a strict household, begs the party to help her join a satyr troupe heading out of the polis.
A funeral pyre is interrupted by the corpse sitting up and walking off — Returned. The mourners are paralyzed with shock.
A festival contest awards a small magic item (uncommon, DM choice) to the winner of a riddling contest (DC 16 Intelligence).
A street prophet of Phenax sells 'true names' on bone tags for 5 gp each. Roughly 1 in 10 is genuinely a true name of a minor spirit.
A pair of philosophers argue whether the gods exist — joining the debate may earn enmity or favor of any god the party defends or attacks.
A returned noble walks brazenly through the agora with two armed retainers (thugs CR 1/2) — daring anyone to stop him. Hoplites watch, unsure.
A magistrate calls the party as expert witnesses in a trial about a creature attack outside the walls — testimony shapes a 100 gp bounty.
A wandering doctor offers to remove a curse for 100 gp — 75% legitimate (lesser restoration), 25% scam.
A high priestess of Ephara invites the party to a private audience: she has dreamed of them and needs their help with a sealed prophecy.
At midnight a single bronze bell tolls thirteen times — a sign of Phenax. Anyone who hears it makes a DC 12 Wisdom save or have a vivid false memory implanted (DM choice).
A wandering hierophant offers free Augury readings in the agora — one true line of guidance per supplicant, no payment accepted.
villages: The village elder greets the party warmly and offers shelter for the night in exchange for tales of the road.
A farmer's plow has unearthed a cracked bronze hoplite helm — he asks the party if it is valuable (worth 25 gp to a temple).
Children chase the party through the village square, asking to see their weapons. Parents intervene with apologies and offers of olives.
2d4 returned (zombie CR 1/4) shamble out of the family graveyard at dusk — a Returned outbreak. The villagers are unarmed.
A wedding feast is in progress — the party is welcomed, fed, and asked to dance. Refusing gives mild offense.
A wolf (CR 1/4) has been raiding the goat pens. The shepherd offers 10 gp to kill it.
A traveling priest of Karametra blesses the fields. He recruits aid for a small ritual that requires fresh olive oil and a song.
Bandits (1d4+1 bandits CR 1/8 + 1 bandit captain CR 2) demand the village's grain stores. The villagers cannot fight back.
A satyr has taken up residence in the village well, drinking the spring dry. He is harmless but won't leave without wine.
A child has fallen into an old cistern. Rescue: DC 13 Athletics + rope; failure means 1d6 falling damage to the child.
The miller's daughter is missing — last seen near the woods. Tracking: DC 15 Survival leads to a satyr courtship (consensual but unsanctioned).
A pig has escaped and is loose in the agora. Catching it: DC 12 Dex check or Animal Handling. Reward: smoked ham for the road.
A leonin trapper passing through offers to sell the party fresh-skinned hides (5 gp each) and rumors of beasts in the area.
The village shrine to Heliod has been desecrated — animal bones and black symbols. Cleansing it: 1 hour ritual + 10 gp in materials.
A traveling tinker offers minor repairs and trinkets. His mule is a polymorphed thief on the run from the next village.
A widow offers a hot meal in exchange for cutting wood for the day — honest work, 5 gp, and a healing draught (one potion of healing).
A pack of 1d4 dire wolves (CR 1) is stalking the outer farms at night.
The village oracle (commoner with one true prophecy) has dreamed of a stranger arriving — and points at one party member. She offers one true if cryptic vision.
A pair of satyrs are stealing wine from the cellar of the village inn. Subduing them (non-lethally) earns the innkeeper's gratitude and free lodging.
A swarm of insects (CR 2) has descended on the crops — Pharika's curse. Driving them off requires fire and 30 minutes of work, no combat.
The village smith is sick. The party can volunteer to keep the forge running (DC 13 Strength + smith's tools) — earns 15 gp and a temporary friend.
A returned villager (zombie CR 1/4) is bound in a barn, still recognizable. The family begs the party to either restore or release him.
A wandering bard takes up residence at the inn and offers a free song to the party in exchange for a story.
A territorial boar (CR 1) is rooting up vegetable gardens. Killing it: 25 gp + fresh pork for a week.
A child's pet pegasus foal (treat as warhorse foal, friendly) has wandered off — find within the day or it is taken by harpies.
A traveling apothecary will sell common potions at half price if the party brings him 5 sprigs of fresh mountain herb (DC 12 Survival to find in 1 hour).
A villager has been accused of theft and is to be stoned at dawn — DC 15 Investigation in 1 hour clears him; the real thief is the constable's son.
A satyr troupe rolls into the village square with wine, pipes, and dancing. They invite the party to perform with them — Charisma performance contest, 10 gp purse.
A returned priest (cult fanatic CR 2) is converting villagers to Phenax in secret meetings. The mayor asks the party to investigate quietly.
A bronze plowshare unearthed in the fields turns out to be a minor relic of Iroas — donating it to the temple earns lasting village goodwill (+2 to all Charisma checks in this village forever).
A pair of leonin hunters pass through with a wagon full of pelts; they trade 1 pelt for 10 gp and gossip.
A young hero of the village (commoner, age 14) begs the party to take him as a squire on the road.
The well water has gone bitter overnight — Pharika's curse or a dead animal in the well. DC 13 Investigation to determine; 30 min to fix.
A funeral is in progress — a Returned villager was finally laid to rest with full rites. Attending earns the village's quiet respect.
A child claims a 'shining stranger' visited her in dreams. The next morning a single golden olive lies on the porch — a sign of Heliod.
A traveling magus offers minor cantrips for 1 sp a show; he secretly seeks an apprentice from among the village youth.
A wandering centaur (CR 2 but non-hostile) stops to water his hooves and trade news.
The village's prized bull has been killed by a chimera (CR 6 — far too tough under level 5; downgrade to manticore CR 3 or perytons CR 2 ×2 for level 3 parties).
A traveler's wagon has been overturned in the village square — the merchant (commoner) offers 25 gp for help righting it and protecting cargo.
A small shrine to Nylea in a sacred grove just outside the village has been vandalized. The dryad (CR 1) tending it is wounded and asks for help.
A pair of giant rats (CR 1/8) have nested under the temple floor. Cleaning them out: 5 gp + thanks from the priest.
A returned hoplite (skeleton CR 1/4) wanders into the village leading a goat by a rope — he is gentle, confused, and remembers being a farmer here.
The village inn is full because a famous poet is in residence. The poet will give the party a verse-blessing in exchange for travel tales.
A child has built a small altar to Heliod from river stones — touching it grants a tiny boon: 1 temporary HP for the next hour.
Bandits (1 thug CR 1/2 + 2 bandits CR 1/8) are extorting protection money from the inn. The innkeeper begs for help.
A leonin elder offers a single piece of true wisdom in exchange for the party's listening for one full hour (advantage on the next Wisdom save).
The village holds a small festival of Karametra — wreath weaving, fresh bread, and a footrace (DC 13 Athletics, prize: 10 gp and a loaf).
A wandering satyr offers to teach one party member a song that, when sung, calms one beast (treat as one casting of animal friendship per day, lasts 1 week).
A mob of villagers (4d6 commoners) has cornered a Returned in a barn — they want to burn it. The party can intervene.
A pair of pegasi nest on the village watchtower — beautiful but their droppings are a problem. Removing the nest without harming them: DC 14 Animal Handling.
A young centaur runs into the village pursued by harpies (1d4 harpies CR 1). She begs for shelter.
An old hoplite veteran (use veteran CR 3 but friendly) offers to spar with the party — non-lethal, 5 gp stake.
The village's annual sacrifice to Mogis (forbidden) is being conducted in secret by 1d4 cult fanatics (CR 2). The mayor pretends not to know.
A returned grandmother sits on her porch as if nothing has happened. The family insists she is fine. (Wight CR 3 if cornered; otherwise pure sadness.)
A traveling merchant offers to buy any monster parts the party has — 5 gp per claw, 10 gp per fang, 25 gp per intact organ.
A villager's prize chicken has been stolen by a fox. Tracking: DC 12 Survival. Reward: dozen eggs and goodwill.
A wandering priest of Athreos sets up shop at the river crossing, charging 1 obol per soul. He will guide the recently dead's spirits if paid.
A fire breaks out in the smithy — a forge-soot mephit (use magma mephit CR 1/2) has escaped. The smith offers 20 gp to recapture it without damage.
A child has befriended a satyr foundling in the woods. The parents want it driven off; the satyr is harmless but the elders fear it.
A returned hunter (skeleton CR 1/4) wanders into the village square with his bow and a sack of fresh kills. He doesn't speak, only hunts and shares meat.
A wandering oracle gives the party three small bronze tokens — each may be exchanged for one true answer at her shrine in a distant polis.
A village dispute: two farmers claim the same olive grove (DC 14 Persuasion to broker peace, 50 gp reward from whoever wins).
A pack of jackals (use wolves CR 1/4 × 3) raids the henhouse at night.
A young man wants to leave the village and become a hero. His mother begs the party to discourage him — or to take him with them.
A trader's stall sells obsidian beads said to be tears of Erebos. Wearing one grants disadvantage on death saves (DM gentle warning if a player buys one).
A traveling musician offers to teach any party member one folk song that grants advantage on a single performance check (one use).
A village child claims to have seen Heliod walking the road at dawn — adults dismiss it but the road truly smells of warm bronze.
A returned (zombie CR 1/4) is found nailed to the village gate as a warning by a passing inquisitor. The villagers don't know who.
A peryton (CR 2) circles the village at dusk, eyeing the youngest. Driving it off: ranged combat, easy for level 3+.
A traveling merchant offers exotic spices (worth 30 gp/lb) at half price — they were 'liberated' from a temple of Karametra and are spiritually tainted.
A leonin caravan rests outside the village walls. They invite the party to dine on roast goat and share rumors of distant lands.
A village girl has secretly been visiting a satyr in the woods — her father wants her saved; she wants to be left alone.
A bronze sundial in the agora suddenly runs backward for an hour — an omen of Kruphix. Asking why earns one cryptic answer from any local priest.

A pair of children claim to have seen the Cave of Light just past the next ridge — only the worthy can find it (true: it appears for those of pure intent, granting one casting of guidance per day for a week).
A fire has destroyed a granary; villagers blame Returned, but DC 14 Investigation finds it was a jealous farmer.
A wandering knight of Heliod stops at the inn, looking for company on a quest north. He offers to share earnings.
A storm last night uncovered an old hoplite tomb at the edge of the cemetery. Inside: 50 gp grave goods + 1 skeleton CR 1/4 + 1 wight CR 3 (downgrade to single skeleton for very low parties).
A young woman is to be married off to a wealthy elder; she begs the party to help her flee. Her father is the village constable (use veteran CR 3).
A village dog adopts one party member, following them everywhere for the next 1d4 days (use mastiff CR 1/8).
A returned dog (use zombie variant, CR 1/8) follows the party out of town — friendly, harmless, unsettling.
A traveling priest of Pharika asks for help collecting venom from a giant spider's nest (CR 1) just outside town — pays 25 gp.
A child gives a party member a hand-woven good-luck charm. It grants advantage on the next death save (single use).
The village's only well is collapsing. DC 14 Strength (group check) and 2 hours of work to repair. Reward: village goodwill and a roast meal.
A masked stranger (assassin CR 8 — too tough for low parties; downgrade to spy CR 1) is asking about one of the party members in the inn. The innkeeper warns them.
A pair of stirges (CR 1/8 each) attack the inn's chickens at dawn — easy combat for any party.
A wandering bard offers to write the party into a song for free — the song will be sung in this village for a generation, granting +1 to Charisma checks here forever.
A farmer's plow has cracked open a sealed amphora of ancient wine — drinking it grants 1d4 temporary HP and a mild vision of the past.
A pack of 1d4 jackals (use wolves CR 1/4) is bold enough to enter the village square in broad daylight — sign of a deeper hunger in the wilds.
A returned cultist (cult fanatic CR 2) is hiding in a barn, preaching to the youth. Confronting him: combat or social.
A small caravan of refugees from a destroyed village asks for protection on the road south — 3 days' travel, no pay, but lasting goodwill.
A village child has gone missing in the woods. Tracking: DC 14 Survival; she is found cold but alive after 4 hours. Reward: village's lifelong gratitude.
A festival of Iroas — a small wrestling tournament with a 10 gp purse and a bronze laurel. Entry is free.
A satyr offers to swap a 'small favor' for a flask of fine wine. He will deliver one true rumor about any nearby location within a day.
A wandering oracle reads the entrails of a chicken and announces one party member must travel north before the next full moon, or suffer ill fortune.
A returned hero of the village (use wight CR 3) sits on the steps of the temple at dawn, weeping. The villagers don't know what to do.
A young priestess of Karametra invites the party to bless the harvest — a ritual that grants advantage on the next Wisdom (Survival) check for finding food.
A pair of harpies (CR 1 each) nest in the bell tower of the village shrine — driving them off: ranged combat or DC 14 Animal Handling to coax them out.
A village dispute over a stolen olive press becomes a tense standoff. The party can broker (DC 15 Insight + Persuasion) or watch a feud begin.
At dawn the entire village wakes to find a single golden olive on every doorstep — a sign of Heliod, and a quiet blessing on the village for one season.
The village's oldest olive tree blooms out of season — Karametra's blessing. Anyone who sleeps under it heals 1d4 extra HP on their next long rest, once per person.
